IMG_20260503_190752_005.jpg: Ferrari 512 TR, still perfect from the day it left Maranello. The 512 TR took everything Testarossa and sharpened it, better handling, more power, and those wider rear arches doing all the talking. Would you take the Testarossa or the 512 TR?
IMG_20260503_190904_828.jpg: The front arch Enzo signed off as his final Ferrari. Every vent, every curve on this car was obsessed over before he passed in 1988. It's raw in a way modern Ferraris simply aren't allowed to be anymore. What do you reckon is the best detail on an F40?
IMG_20260503_191325_342.jpg: Four titanium pipes and deep blue carbon-fibre. The Zonda's rear end is art before it's engineering. Horacio Pagani named this car after the wind that crosses the Andes, and somehow that feels about right when you're stood behind one. What's your favourite detail on a Zonda?
IMG_20260503_191441_067.jpg: Porsche 911 Le Mans Clubsport, clean and silver under the show lights. This is the road car that wore racing DNA on its sleeve, built for drivers who wanted something a bit more purposeful than the standard 911. It's a rare one to see up close at a show. Would you go Le Mans Clubsport or a standard 964 if you had the choice?
IMG_20260503_191611_130.jpg: Two 911 Turbo 3.0s, black and silver, side by side. The car that gave the widow-maker its name. Lift off mid-corner and the rear swing would catch most drivers completely off guard. Have you ever driven something that genuinely kept you on edge?
IMG_20260503_191840_818.jpg: Blue McLaren Senna, up close and real. Named after Ayrton, developed with his family's blessing, this is as close to a spiritual successor as McLaren could build. The road car that was never supposed to be comfortable. What's the most Senna moment you remember from his time in F1?
IMG_20260503_192032_018.jpg: Pink Bentley, no apologies. The "UNLEADED IN HERE / CAFFEINE BACK THERE" wrap sums up the owner's priorities pretty neatly. You'd never mistake this one in a car park. What's the best custom wrap you've ever seen in the wild?
IMG_20260503_192220_007.jpg: 40 RED. The plate does the talking on this one. The F40 was the last car Enzo signed off on before he died in 1988, and you can feel the intention in every line of it. Raw, unfiltered, built to go fast and nothing else. What's the first thing that draws your eye on an F40?
IMG_20260503_192442_806.jpg: Grey McLaren nose, up close and personal. That low snout is all business, and the Sligo plates make it one of the more unexpected places you'd expect to find one. How many McLarens do you reckon are registered in Ireland right now?
IMG_20260503_192712_783.jpg: That blue iridescent paint shifting over raw carbon-fibre. The Zonda is one of those cars that genuinely looks different depending on where the light hits it. A Mercedes-AMG V12 under the bonnet, handbuilt in Modena, and not a single straight line anywhere on the body. What's your favourite Zonda variant?
IMG_20260503_234051_444.jpg: That blue-grey on a GT3 RS is something else. Custodian Classics and Porsche Centre Dublin have turned this one out beautifully, and it looks just right in that colour. What spec would you go for if you were building your GT3 RS?
IMG_20260503_234528_510.jpg: White BMW M1, those M-sport stripes doing all the talking. BMW built exactly one mid-engined production road car, and this is it. A joint project with Lamborghini that ended up defining an era of homologation specials. Would you keep it standard or is that stripe the only way to have one?
IMG_20260503_234814_290.jpg: That dark green 911 up front is demanding all the attention. Two early cars from Custodian Classics, the air-cooled generation that set the template for everything that came after. They still look right parked next to each other like this. Which colour would you go for if you were choosing between these two?
IMG_20260503_235252_421.jpg: Silver Porsche 911 and yellow Giulia GT, side by side. Two of the most characterful cars to come out of the 1960s, and somehow they look even better together. Which one are you taking home?
IMG_20260503_235536_754.jpg: Yellow Ferrari 275 GTB, and it's stopping people in their tracks. This was the car that changed how Ferrari approached handling, the first to get fully independent rear suspension on all four corners. It shows, too, because the 275 GTB drove unlike anything else wearing the prancing horse badge at the time. Which era of Ferrari road car do you think produced the best driving machines?
IMG_20260504_000957_371.jpg: Four round headlights and that shield badge, the GTV needs nothing else. This is old Alfa at its best, a car that wore its identity without trying too hard. The colour, the stance, the whole thing just works. What's your favourite era of Alfa Romeo design?
IMG_20260504_001453_491.jpg: Four round headlights and that classic Giulia GT nose. Yellow suits this car better than almost any other colour, framing those proportions just right. What's your favourite era of Alfa Romeo design?
IMG_20260504_001721_116.jpg: Bonnet up on the New Beetle RSi. That 3.2-litre VR6 demands a proper look. Volkswagen only made 250 of these, and seeing one in full Deane Motor Company livery with the engine on show is a proper treat. Did you even know VW built a hot Beetle with a VR6?
IMG_20260504_001947_200.jpg: That FORD grille staring you down. The Mk1 Transit in red, and those letters across the front mean business. It's hard to think of a van that shaped working life in Britain and Ireland more than this one. What's the most memorable thing you've seen one of these carrying?
IMG_20260504_002330_444.jpg: Yellow 964 with that J999 CUP plate. This is the generation that brought all-wheel drive to the 911 for the first time, and Porsche managed to do it without losing any of what makes the car. That colour suits it better than it has any right to. Air-cooled or water-cooled, which era of 911 does it for you?
IMG_20260504_002654_112.jpg: That whale tail. There's no mistaking a 930 from behind. Custodian Classics had this silver example looking spot on, and that rear end still turns heads forty-odd years on. What's your favourite generation of air-cooled 911?
IMG_20260504_003126_589.webp: The Zonda's diffuser and exhausts, up close. Pagani hand-built every detail on this car from day one in 1999, and you can feel the obsession just looking at it. What's your favourite detail on the Zonda?
DSC_4855-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Cadillac #38 under the lights in the Le Mans pit lane. That black and gold Hertz livery looks the business in person, especially at night when the whole garage is buzzing with mechanics working against the clock. What's your favourite pit lane livery from this year's race?
DSC_4862-Enhanced-NR.jpg: #38 Cadillac, pit box at Le Mans 2026. Two Cadillac operations side by side, the WTR #101 right next door. There's something about the pit lane between stints that shows the scale of what it takes to run at this level. Which team's pit box setup catches your eye most at Le Mans?
DSC_4870-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Yellow Keating Corvette Z06 GT3.R sitting pretty on Goodyear rubber at Le Mans 2026. The #33 looked sharp all weekend and Goodyear's rubber was doing serious work across the 24 hours. How do you rate Goodyear's return to Le Mans this year?
DSC_4871-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Keating Motorsport Corvette #33 in the pit lane at Le Mans 2026. That yellow livery under the Rolex signage is hard to miss in a sea of machinery. What do you make of the American muscle running in LMGT3 against the Europeans?
DSC_4875-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Valkyrie AMR Pro. Green and mean at Le Mans.

Under The Heart of Racing banner in the pit lane, this thing revs past 11,100rpm, a number that barely makes sense in a racing car. How many times did you hear it on the Mulsanne this year?
DSC_4876-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Dark green #007 in the pit complex, Le Mans 2026. There's something about the Thor Team Aston Martin that looks right at home here, that colour under the fluorescent lights almost glowing against the concrete. Bond references write themselves with this one, but the car earns the number on its own merits. What do you reckon, does the #007 plate add to the occasion or is it a bit of a distraction?
DSC_4879-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Michelin chicane glowing at 2am, Le Mans 2026. Taillights disappear into the dark beyond the arch while the circuit comes alive around you. What's your favourite spot to watch the night hours at Le Mans?
DSC_4881-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Ford #88 sitting in the pit lane at Le Mans 2026. That blue livery looks sharp under the circuit lights, and you can feel the tension of the whole weekend in a single pit stop. Are you backing Ford to make their mark at this year's race?
DSC_4891-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Le Mans after dark, framed by Michelin and Rolex. There's something about the 24 Heures at night that no other race can touch, the glow of the branding, the hum of machinery that never stops. Which hour of the night do you think is the hardest on the drivers?
DSC_4894-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Akkodis Lexus RC F GT3, pit lane, Le Mans 2026. The GT3 class at this race is relentless, and the Lexus boys have their work cut out surrounded by Porsches and Ferraris. Are you a fan of seeing manufacturer GT3 cars like this at Le Mans, or do you prefer the hypercars stealing the headlines?
DSC_4896-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Tower Events P2, stationary in the Le Mans pit lane. That orange and blue livery is hard to miss even when the car's doing nothing, which won't last long. What's your favourite LMP2 livery from this year's grid?
DSC_4897-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Valkyrie, boxed in between #007 and #009 at Le Mans. The Heart of Racing outfit brought two of these wild machines to the 24 Hours in 2026, and the pit lane felt different with them in it. Which of the two cars do you think had the better race?
DSC_4899-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Valkyrie in the pit lane at Le Mans. Aston Martin's #009 entry from The Heart of Racing is one of the most extreme cars ever built, and here it is doing the unglamorous work between stints. How do you think the Valkyrie held up over the full 24 hours?
DSC_4903-Enhanced-NR.jpg: #93 Peugeot 9X8 in the TotalEnergies pit at Le Mans 2026. When this car first raced in 2022 it turned up without a rear wing, which felt like a proper statement of intent. Three years on and it's still one of the most distinctive shapes on the grid. Do you think the wingless concept was brave engineering or a step too far?
DSC_4905-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Three Aston Martin bays, one frame, zero wasted space. The #23 Vantage GT3 sits alongside two Valkyrie hypercars, and the contrast between them tells the whole story of what Aston brought to Le Mans 2026. GT3 grit on one side, prototype ambition on the other. Which project would you rather be working on this week?
DSC_4907-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Valkyrie stops, the crew moves. #009 under the Thor Team gantry, every second counting. You forget how low this car sits until you see the mechanics working around it. What's your take on the Valkyrie as a race car?
DSC_4910-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Two Astons, side by side in the Le Mans pit lane. The Vantage GT3 #23 and the Valkyrie AMR Pro #009 sharing the same garage row, which doesn't happen every day. Heart of Racing brought both cars to the fight this year, from GT3 all the way up to the Hypercar class. Which one would you rather be watching through the night?
DSC_4912-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Two Valkyries in the same pit lane. #009 and #007 sit side by side in the Thor Team garage at Le Mans 2026, the AMR Pro in its natural habitat. Not many teams can say they rolled two of these out at the same race. Which of the two would you want behind the wheel of?
DSC_4915-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Valkyrie in its pit box, Le Mans 2026. The Heart of Racing #009 sits under the Thor Team gantry, the whole operation ready to send it back out into the dark. There's something about seeing this car up close that makes you appreciate just how wild a road car it is to be racing here. Did you get to see the Valkyrie in person this year?
DSC_4922-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Both Astons in the box at the same time. Thor Team brings #007 and #008 in together under the Le Mans night sky, a full double pit stop executed in the dark. The coordination it takes to run two cars through simultaneously, with two crews, two sets of tyres, two strategies, is something else. Which Vantage are you on, #007 or #008?
DSC_4924-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Double bay, double trouble for Thor Team at Le Mans 2026. The #009 sits front and centre with #007 right next door, and there's something about seeing two Aston Martins pitted side by side that just feels right at this circuit. Which of the two cars do you want to see on the top step?
DSC_4926-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Aston Martin pit complex, Le Mans 2026. The #009 sits front and centre with the Rolex boards ticking overhead, the whole operation spread out behind it. There's something about seeing a team's full setup like this that reminds you just how much goes into running a car for 24 hours. Which team's pit complex do you think looks the most impressive at Le Mans?
DSC_4931-Enhanced-NR.jpg: United Autosports #22 and Manthey Racing, pit lane neighbours. Two very different programmes side by side, that's the magic of La Sarthe. Which team were you watching closest this year?
DSC_4933-Enhanced-NR.jpg: United Autosports #22 sits in the pit lane, Manthey next door. Le Mans 2026, between stints, and the garages are buzzing with that quiet intensity you only get here. Two very different programmes, side by side in the same strip of tarmac. Which team's setup would you rather be standing in right now?
DSC_4941-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Toyota GR010 through the trees, marshals watching on. There's something timeless about this section of the circuit, the light filtering through the canopy as the cars flash past. Toyota haven't won here since 2022, and every lap feels like a reminder of what they're chasing. Do you think they can get back to the top step?
DSC_4944-Enhanced-NR.jpg: LMGT3 pack racing through Le Mans, nobody giving an inch. The dark blue and yellow car holds the lead but there's a queue of hungry rivals right on its tail. This is what the GT class is all about at the 24 Hours. Which manufacturer were you rooting for in LMGT3 this year?
DSC_4952-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Car #9 cuts through the damp at Le Mans 2026. The red and black Florian Racing Mind livery looks sharp against a wet circuit, Motul backing on the flanks as the LMP3 gets on with the job. Damp conditions at La Sarthe separate the confident from the cautious. How do you think the LMP3 class shapes up against the big prototypes in traffic?
DSC_4959-Enhanced-NR.jpg: #10 Aston returning to the pits with armco across its nose. That's the raw reality of Le Mans, a car limping home wearing the barrier it just collected. How much damage do you reckon it took to bring all that fencing with it?
DSC_4960-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #54 Porsche in Purpose colours, out on circuit at Le Mans. White and dark grey looks proper clean through the curves, that flat-six doing its thing in the GT3 class. What's your favourite livery running at this year's race?
DSC_4969-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Safety Car B on circuit, Le Mans 2026. The matte dark grey and red Porsche 911 with that amber light bar spinning on the roof is a sight that gets everyone's attention, whether you're watching from the stands or glued to a timing screen. When the safety car comes out at Le Mans, what's your first reaction?
DSC_4970-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Gulf colours on the #77 McLaren at Le Mans Cup 2026. That blue-grey and orange combo has looked right on a racing car since the 1960s, and the 720S wears it well. Does Gulf have the best livery history in motorsport?
DSC_4974-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Ferrari GT3 leads, a green and gold car right on its tail. Multi-class racing is something else. GT and prototype battles happening all over the circuit at the same time. Which class do you find yourself watching most closely at Le Mans?
DSC_4975-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Hypercar, LMP2 and GT3 all bunched up under the yellows. Nobody racing, but the tension is still there, everyone watching gaps through the dappled light. How many classes can you spot in this pack?
DSC_4976-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Hypercar and GT3 side by side through Village under yellows. The gap in performance between these two classes is enormous, yet here they are sharing the same patch of tarmac at the world's greatest race. Which class do you find more interesting to watch at Le Mans?
DSC_4977-Enhanced-NR.jpg: A pack of prototypes through Maison Blanche, tricolour watching over them. This section always delivers the best angles at Le Mans, cars pushed flat out before the run to the Ford Chicanes. Which car in the pack caught your eye?
DSC_4984-Enhanced-NR.jpg: LMP3 #23 hammering through the bend, headlights cutting the Le Mans night. That white, black and orange livery looks sharp under the lights, the kind of car that quietly earns your respect across 24 hours. Are you a fan of the LMP3 class or do you prefer watching the big prototypes?
DSC_4986-Enhanced-NR.jpg: White and blue holds the line, pink right on its tail. That gap is closing fast and there's nothing between them but commitment and tyre choice. Which prototype caught your eye first?
DSC_4987-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Pack racing through the corner at Le Mans 2026. The white and dark blue car holds the line ahead, with that pink prototype right on its tail and not giving an inch. This is where races are won and lost, inches from the barrier at race pace. Who do you reckon comes out ahead by the next straight?
DSC_4989-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Five LMP3s stacked into the corner, nobody giving an inch. The #35 and #55 are door to door at the front, the rest breathing down their necks. This is where Le Mans 2026 gets decided in the LMP3 class. Which car do you think comes out on top?
DSC_4994-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #7 Mascot Workwear LMP3 in full flight at Le Mans. That white, blue and green livery is one of the cleaner ones in the field this year. Which Le Mans Cup livery has caught your eye so far?
DSC_4995-Enhanced-NR.jpg: #33 Aston Martin punching out of the corner, prototype on its tail. That green, white and orange livery looks right at home in the French countryside. LMGT3 racing at Le Mans is something else when the big prototypes are mixing it up behind you. How do you rate Aston's chances in GT this year?
DSC_4996-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #11 Aston Martin, that green and lime livery unmissable. Le Mans GT racing doesn't get much more British than this, a proper factory effort on the biggest stage. What do you think of Aston's colour scheme this year?
DSC_4997-Enhanced-NR.jpg: LMP3 #183 catching the last of the light at Le Mans 2026. That white and red livery glows against the dusk, headlights already doing the work as the circuit dims. Do you prefer watching Le Mans in the day or once it gets dark?
DSC_4999-Enhanced-NR.jpg: #34 Ligier JS P320 in green and yellow at Le Mans 2026. That RACEBORN 75 livery looks right at home on the Circuit de la Sarthe, the kind of colour scheme that catches your eye through the trees. What's your favourite LMP3 livery from this year's race?
DSC_5001-Enhanced-NR.jpg: BWT pink through the Le Mans countryside. The #9 Ligier JS P320 is hard to miss out there, that colour cuts through everything. LMP3 racing at its best, close and relentless all through the 24 hours. Which class do you think makes Le Mans the most entertaining to watch?
DSC_5002-Enhanced-NR.jpg: BWT Porsche #8 all-pink through the corner at Le Mans 2026. The marshal behind the barrier watches on, another car goes by without a second glance from him. You wonder how many thousands of cars he's seen pass that spot over the years. Pink liveries at Le Mans, love them or hate them?
DSC_5004-Enhanced-NR.jpg: LMP3 door-to-door at Le Mans, nobody giving an inch. The #5 and #58 are right on top of each other, two very different liveries but the same mission. This is where the next generation of prototype drivers prove themselves on the biggest stage in motorsport. Which of these two do you think held the position?
DSC_5005-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #14 Mercedes-AMG GT3 skulls up at Le Mans 2026. That Bartom livery in black is one of the most striking on the grid this year, the skull motif doing exactly what it's meant to do. Which livery from Le Mans 2026 caught your eye the most?
DSC_5006-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Atlantis #67 into the night, leading at Le Mans 2026. The white and red prototype right on the limit, two rivals breathing down its neck. Which car were you watching in this battle?
DSC_5008-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #42 Vimana LMP3, dark carbon, mean and low. Le Mans Cup puts some seriously sharp-looking machinery on track, and this one catches the eye every time it rolls by. What's your take on dark liveries versus bright colours on race cars?
DSC_5009-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Ferrari 296 GT3 #55 carrying the tricolore through Le Mans. That red against the Circuit de la Sarthe barriers is something else. The 296 GT3 has been turning heads in GT3 this year. Do you think Ferrari can take the class win in 2026?
DSC_5011-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Gulf colours on a McLaren at Le Mans. The #77 720S LMGT3 is wearing that iconic orange and blue like it was always meant to be there. Does any livery in motorsport carry more history than Gulf?
DSC_5012-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Ferrari 296 LMGT3 #17 sitting in the Le Mans pit lane,2026. The red and white livery with GUESS branding looks sharp under the garage lights, and this car had plenty of eyes on it all week. How do you rate the 296 GT3 as a Le Mans package?
DSC_5013-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #77 McLaren in Gulf colours, pit lane at Le Mans. That papaya and blue livery looks properly at home under the lights, with Kessel Racing tucked in close behind. Which Gulf livery do you think has looked best at Le Mans this year?
DSC_5014-Enhanced-NR.jpg: That Gulf roundel on slate grey and orange. The McLaren GT3 sitting in the pit lane at Le Mans 2026, and that livery does all the talking. Gulf and Le Mans just belong together. Which Gulf livery from any era is your favourite?
DSC_5016-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The full Hypercar field, side by side before the start. Ferraris, Toyotas, Porsches, Caddies, all sitting quietly before 24 hours of chaos. There's something about this moment, the calm before it all kicks off, that gets you every time. Which manufacturer are you backing this year?
DSC_5017-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Pink in the pit lane at Le Mans 2026. The RWR Oreca 07 #30 sits beneath the Michelin gantry, and that livery is hard to miss in a sea of silver and black. Do you think bold colours like this make LMP2 more interesting to watch?
DSC_5018-Enhanced-NR.jpg: LMP3 #43 cutting through the sunshine at Le Mans 2026. That yellow and green DEKO livery is hard to miss on circuit, catching the light perfectly on one of the world's most famous stretches of tarmac. How does LMP3 compare to the top class for you, is it underrated?
DSC_5019-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #43 hugging the pit wall at Le Mans 2026. That green and yellow livery is impossible to miss as it threads through the corridor between barrier and tarmac. How close is too close to the wall for your nerves?
DSC_5020-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Traflux #70 sitting pretty in the pit lane at Le Mans 2026. The yellow and orange LMP3 catches the eye, with the Purpose Porsche GT3 lurking just behind. Two very different programmes, same pit lane chaos. Which class do you prefer watching at Le Mans, LMP3 or GT3?
DSC_5021-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Vimana #42 in the pit lane at Le Mans. That dark charcoal and red stripe looking sharp under the garage lights. LMP3 is where so many drivers cut their teeth at this race, and there's something great about seeing a private jets brand on the side of a Le Mans car. Did you spot many LMP3 runners out on track this year?
DSC_5022-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The skull-and-crossbones Porsche is in the pit lane at Le Mans. Bartone Bros bringing some pirate energy to the GT3 class with car #4, and honestly it suits the place, a circuit that swallows cars and crews whole over 24 hours. What's your favourite livery in this year's GT3 field?
DSC_5024-Enhanced-NR.jpg: BWT #8 in all its pink glory, pit lane Le Mans 2026. Hard to miss that livery when there's a red car right next door for contrast. Which Le Mans livery from this year stood out most to you?
DSC_5026-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The full grid assembled in the Le Mans pit lane. Prototypes and GT cars side by side, every team making last-minute checks before 24 hours of racing begins. Is there any pre-race atmosphere in motorsport that comes close to this?
DSC_5027-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The full WEC field, stretched back as far as you can see. Hypercars and GT3s packed into the assembly area, everyone waiting for the most famous race in the world to finally get going. Twenty-four hours of this. Which car are you watching most closely this year?
DSC_5030-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The VALEMIK/RDX Ligier sitting pretty in the Le Mans paddock. That black, teal and orange livery looks sharp in the daylight, and the JS P3 is still one of the best-looking LMP3 cars on the grid. How many teams do you reckon make it through the full 24 hours this year?
DSC_5031-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #9 Florja Motorsport LMP3 sits in the Le Mans paddock, that red, orange and black livery looking sharp in the June light. LMP3 is where a lot of young talent cuts its teeth on the Circuit de la Sarthe. Which team in the LMP3 class do you think had the best livery this year?
DSC_5032-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The full Le Mans Cup field stacked up under the gantry. GT3s, Ligiers, and prototypes all queuing for the same strip of tarmac at the most famous circuit in the world. The multi-class grid is what makes this race so good to shoot. Which class do you think is the most exciting to watch at Le Mans?
DSC_5035-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #11 Aston Martin Vantage GT3 sitting pretty at Le Mans. That teal, white and lime green DEFi Le Monde livery looks class under the garage lights, the kind of colour scheme that catches your eye from halfway down the pit lane. Aston GT3s have a real following here. What do you think of this livery?
DSC_5036-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Michelin rubber and the Peugeot lion, millimetres apart at Le Mans. The 9X8 runs on a knife's edge between grip and disaster for 24 hours, and it all starts here at the contact patch. How much do you think about tyres when you're watching an endurance race?
DSC_5037-Enhanced-NR.jpg: GetSpeed's AMG GT3 #14 sitting bold in the Le Mans paddock. That illustrated graphic against the matte black livery is a proper head-turner, and the American flag on the roof tells you exactly where this crew's loyalties lie. Which GT3 car has your favourite livery from Le Mans 2026?
DSC_5041-Enhanced-NR.jpg: #49 High Class Racing on the charge at Le Mans 2026. Rackstraw and Hove in that clean white, red and blue livery, doing what LMP3 does best, keeping it close and keeping it honest. Are you a fan of the LMP3 class or do you need a bit more power in your life?
DSC_5043-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Traflux #70 sitting pretty in the Le Mans pit lane. The yellow and black Ligier JS P3 is hard to miss, and that Logitech G branding is right at home on a racing prototype. The Le Mans Cup field doesn't get the same spotlight as the Hypercar class, but the racing is just as fierce. Which class do you think produces the better wheel-to-wheel action at Le Mans?
DSC_5051-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Manthey in the pit lane at Le Mans 2026. The white 911 GT3R sits ready, that rear wing doing exactly what it looks like it should. Porsche and Manthey together at La Sarthe is always worth watching. Do you think this is the year they take the GTE/GT3 honours?
DSC_5060-Enhanced-NR.jpg: GT car punches under the Goodyear bridge, headlights cutting the grey. Le Mans 2026 doesn't do sunshine, it does atmosphere, and there's plenty of it here. Which corner on the Circuit de la Sarthe gives you the best light for a shot like this?
DSC_5061-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Three prototypes through the Goodyear bridge corner. The blue car leads, orange and white right on its tail, and at Le Mans that gap can close in a heartbeat. Which of the three lines do you think was the quickest through there?
DSC_5062-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Yellow GT machine through the Goodyear arch at Le Mans 2026. The flat grey sky gives it that classic La Sarthe atmosphere, no sun needed. Which GT car had you watching closest this year?
DSC_5063-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Two prototypes under the Goodyear bridge, grey sky overhead. That iconic bridge has framed so many battles over the decades, and here it is doing it again at Le Mans 2026. The red car leads, but there's nothing between them. Which is your favourite Le Mans landmark to photograph from?
DSC_5064-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Red leads blue under the Goodyear bridge, Le Mans 2026. Two GT cars hunting each other through one of the circuit's most iconic landmarks, the light barely enough to read the tyres. Which GT class battle had you on the edge of your seat this year?
DSC_5066-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Panis Racing under pressure through the corner, yellow breathing down their neck. The LMP2 battle at Le Mans 2026 was relentless, with rivals never more than a nose away all race long. Which LMP2 team had your attention this year?
DSC_5069-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #38 Oreca 07 cutting through the Le Mans night, headlights burning hard past the barriers. LMP2 at this hour is a different beast, the drivers digging deep while the rest of the world sleeps. How many hours into the race does the night stint hit hardest for you?
DSC_5070-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Mercedes-AMG GT3 framed through the fence at Le Mans, 3am. There's something about shooting through the wire at night that strips everything back, just the car, the lights, and the circuit. What's your favourite spot to watch or photograph Le Mans from?
DSC_5071-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Valkyrie through the fence at Le Mans. That dark green Aston Martin AMR Pro looks like something from another world on circuit, and you're reminded just how close the spectators get to these cars here. Have you ever watched Le Mans trackside, and if so, where did you stand?
DSC_5072-Enhanced-NR.jpg: CrowdStrike Racing at Le Mans 2026 in that dark grey livery. LMP2 at La Sarthe is a different kind of battle, fighting for class honours on the same circuit as the Hypercars, lap after lap through the night. Which LMP2 squad were you watching closest this year?
DSC_5073-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Alpine #35 through the fence, headlights cutting the dark. You don't get an angle like this without the wire between you and something doing 300kph down the Mulsanne. Alpine know this place well, they won it outright back in 1978. Could 2026 be their year again?
DSC_5074-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Ferrari 296 LMGT3 in that Richard Mille livery at Le Mans. The GT3 class at this race is always stacked, and Ferrari's entry looks the part. Which LMGT3 car had your eye this year?
DSC_5075-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Richard Mille's LMH hypercar flat out at La Sarthe. That HY badge is doing real work out here, hybrid power pushing it through every corner of the 24 hours. Do you think the LMH regulations have produced the best-looking top-class cars we've ever seen at Le Mans?
DSC_5076-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #58 McLaren 720S GT3 Evo threading through Le Mans 2026. Shot through the fence, which is just part of the experience at a circuit this size. The Korloff and Goodyear livery works really well on this car. Did you make it to Le Mans this year?
DSC_5086-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The #79 Mercedes-AMG GT3 sitting pretty in the Le Mans pit lane. That CrowdStrike silver and green livery looks sharp under the garage lights, and you know the crew have been over every inch of this car. GT3 racing at Le Mans is its own world within the race. Which GT3 livery from this year's grid was your favourite?
DSC_5096-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Alpine in full flight at Le Mans, elf colours and all. There's something special about this car at this circuit, a French manufacturer back at the top of endurance racing 47 years after their overall win. Do you think Alpine can go all the way in 2026?
DSC_4436-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Chalk Grey GT4 parked up next to a yellow tent. There's something brilliant about seeing a naturally aspirated 4.0-litre flat-six sitting in a camping paddock like any other weekend away. This 2022 718 Cayman GT4 makes the neighbours look well sorted. What's the best thing you've spotted in a paddock car park?
DSC_4443-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Three generations of sports car obsession rolling through the paddock. A GT-R, an Emira and a 911 Cab in the same convoy is the kind of thing that makes a track day worth showing up for. Mizuno's bruiser leads the pack, which feels about right. Which one are you jumping in?
DSC_4452-Enhanced-NR.jpg: That wide rear stance says everything before you've even seen the badge. The R35 GT-R in pearl white is one of those cars that still turns heads nearly two decades on, and D4 XDN wears it well. Twin circles, four letters, a reputation that needs no introduction. Which generation GT-R is your favourite?
DSC_4455-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Rosso Corsa under open skies, exactly as Ferrari intended. The California was a bit of a departure for Maranello when it arrived in 2008, front-engined and fitted with a folding hardtop, but in this colour it's hard to argue with the result. Do you think the California gets the respect it deserves, or does it still live in the shadow of Ferrari's mid-engined cars?
DSC_4457-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Two very different ideas of what a sports car should be. Ferrari's California brought the retractable hardtop to Maranello's lineup, while the 718 Cayman GT4 keeps it ruthlessly focused on the driver. Side by side on the grass, they're a proper conversation starter. Which one are you choosing for a weekend drive?

#Ferrari #FerrariCalifornia #Porsche #CaymanGT4 #718CaymanGT4 #CarShow #TrackMarc #PorschevsFerrari #ClassicMeetsModern #GrandTourer #SportsCar #IrishMotorsport #CarPhotography
DSC_4458-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Those ant decals on a Widebody Challenger are a proper statement. The flared haunches, dark multi-spokes, and a flash of orange caliper behind them make this SRT build hard to walk past at a show. Whose custom livery has caught your eye lately?

#DodgeChallenger #WidebodyChallenger #ScatPack #HellcatWidebody #MuscleCarShow #CarShow #CustomLivery #AmericanMuscle #trackmarcdotcom
DSC_4504-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Red F82 M4, and a TVR lurking in the background. Track days have a way of drawing out the good stuff, and ST17 RMW is looking the part with that M Performance screen banner. The F82 generation brought the M4 name to the two-door body for the first time back in 2014, and they're still turning heads at events like this. Which would you take out first, the BMW or the TVR?
DSC_4546-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Le Mans livery on a road car hits different in the daylight. This 992 is wearing the 24 Heures du Mans 2026 graphics and it's hard not to stop and stare, even on static display. Porsche's eighth-generation 911 has never looked more purposeful standing still. Would you keep it road-registered or put it straight into a collection?
DSC_4554-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Two Aventadors parked up, and neither one is trying to blend in. Gunmetal grey and dark olive side by side under a marquee, the V12 flagship in two of its quieter shades, which somehow makes the angular bodywork hit even harder. Which colour works better on the Aventador for you?
DSC_4555-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Those quad exhausts tell you everything you need to know. This Swiss-registered 911 Carrera 4S Cab is wearing the wider rear bodywork that sets the all-wheel-drive model apart from its siblings, and it looks all the better for it. The canton Vaud plates are a nice touch at an outdoor show. Would you take the roof off in this weather, or leave it up and just enjoy the sound?
DSC_4558-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Two very different philosophies of fast, parked side by side on the grass. The Lotus keeps it simple, all louvres and lightweight attitude, while the GT-R NISMO sits behind it with those yellow Brembo calipers quietly making a point. Both right, just in completely different ways. Which school of thought do you belong to?
DSC_4560-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
700 horsepower sitting perfectly still, daring you to look away.

The 991.2 GT2 RS in GT Silver Metallic is one of those cars that commands the whole space around it. Wide arches, those yellow calipers, the magnesium wheels, it all adds up to something that feels more race car than road car, even parked up at a show.

Would you daily it if you could?

#Porsche #911GT2RS #GT2RS #991 #PorscheGT #FlatSix #TrackMarc #MotorsportPhotography #CarShow #PorscheLife
DSC_4563-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Seven hundred horses, sat perfectly still in Chalk white. The 991.2 GT2 RS is the kind of car you walk past twice, then come back a third time just to check it's real. The rear end alone tells you everything you need to know about what Porsche was thinking in 2018. What's your take on Chalk as a colour for this car?
DSC_4564-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Silver, low, and absolutely purposeful in the French sun. The Alfa Romeo 4C is one of those cars that looks almost too serious for a Sunday show, all carbon tub and composite body with barely 900 kg to its name. It's a reminder that Alfa could still make something properly raw when they wanted to. What's your take on the 4C, underrated gem or forgotten footnote?
DSC_4572-Enhanced-NR.jpg: That rear wing means business, even standing still. The Racing Yellow Cayman GT4 is one of those cars that looks aggressive from every angle, and the yellow calipers peeking through those dark spokes are a proper touch. Same naturally aspirated flat-six as the 911 GT3 under the lid, which tells you everything you need to know. Would you take it on track or keep it for the road?
DSC_4573-Enhanced-NR.jpg: That V12 sits there like it owns the entire field. The Aventador was Lamborghini's answer to anyone who thought the Murciélago had said everything there was to say, and 700 naturally aspirated horses later, it's hard to argue. No race numbers, no livery, just a silver road car doing what these things do best. Have you ever heard one of these start up in person?
DSC_4576-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Yellow GT4 and Aventurine Red M3, two very different approaches to the same obsession. The Cayman GT4 brings that naturally aspirated flat-six straight from Porsche's GT programme, while the G80 M3 sits alongside it looking every bit as purposeful on the grass. Two cars, two philosophies, one very good afternoon at a UK show. Which one are you taking home?

#Porsche #GT4 #718CaymanGT4 #BMWM3 #G80M3 #CarShow #TrackCars #PorscheGT #FlatSix #PerformanceCars #UKCarScene #trackmarcdotcom
DSC_4578-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Slate grey, gold wheels, and absolutely nothing to prove. This 992 GT3 Touring is doing the sleeper act perfectly, hiding 510 naturally aspirated horses behind that clean, wing-free tail. It's one of the most restrained ways to have a proper GT3, and honestly it's all the better for it. Gold wheels on grey, love it or leave it?

#Porsche #GT3Touring #992 #PorscheGT3 #911Touring #CarShow #PorscheClub #FlatSix #TrackMarc #Concours #MotorsportPhotography
DSC_4580-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
That Carmine Red Targa bar says everything. The 992 Targa carries a design that dates back to 1967, and Porsche still hasn't found a reason to change the formula. The fixed stainless hoop, the round headlight signature, another 911 lurking in the background just to remind you there's a whole family of them. Which generation of Targa is your favourite?

#Porsche #911Targa #992 #PorscheTarga #CarmineRed #CarShow #Porsche911 #trackmarcdotcom
DSC_4583-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Daytona Grey R8, those LED strips cutting clean across the nose. The first-gen R8 still turns heads a decade on, partly because Audi quietly built it on the same aluminium spaceframe as the Lamborghini Gallardo. R88 MHO was sitting pretty in the sunshine, and honestly it looked right at home. V8 or V10, which would you have?

#AudiR8 #R8 #Audi #CarShow #DaytonaGrey #MidEngine #TrackMarcDotCom #CarPhotography #AudiLove #ClassicModern
DSC_4585-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Matte grey R8 in the queue, and it still turns every head.

The first-gen Audi R8 doesn't need to shout. That 4.2-litre V8, shared with the Lamborghini Gallardo, does the talking once the revs build. This one in matte dark grey looks every bit as good as the day it left Neckarsulm.

Still your pick for best-looking mid-engined car of its era?

#AudiR8 #R8V8 #AudiR8V8 #CarShow #Audi #SupercarSunday #TrackMarcDotCom #MotorsportPhotography #CarPhotography #ConcoursDElegance
DSC_4589-Enhanced-NR.jpg: That carbon wing tells you everything you need to know. This white GT-R Nismo sits on static display but it doesn't need to move to make a statement, the LM 2026 roundel on the rear quarter adding a nice touch of character. The R35 Nismo is Nissan's motorsport division doing what they do best, and even at a standstill it looks like it's doing about 80. Would you daily one if you could? #GTR #NissanGTR #Nismo #R35 #CarShow #JDM #TrackMarc
DSC_4590-Enhanced-NR.jpg: That Le Mans badge on a five-cylinder road car hits different. Audi's RS3 Sportback in Tango Red, dressed head to toe in 24 Heures du Mans livery, the kind of thing you'd expect to see in a paddock, not parked on the grass at a show. The 2.5-litre turbo five under that bonnet has been the heartbeat of Audi Sport road cars for years, and it still earns that branding. Would you keep it standard or is the livery the only way to run one?
DSC_4592-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
700 horses parked on grass, and it still looks like it wants to bite you.

The 991.2 GT2 RS in GT Silver with that carbon bonnet and red interior is a proper wolf in wolf's clothing. Yellow calipers, wide arches, and a twin-turbo flat-six that made this the most powerful road-legal 911 of its generation.

What's your take on the carbon bonnet vents, love them or leave them?

#Porsche #GT2RS #911GT2RS #PorscheRS #991 #FlatSix #CarShow #PorscheLife #TrackMarc #MotorsportPhotography
DSC_4600-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
The GT-R Nismo sits still, but it doesn't look at rest. That carbon bonnet, the gold-centred wheels, and a Le Mans sticker on the door tell you this one's been built with something to prove. The Nismo variant lifts straight from Nissan's GT3 programme, and you can feel that intent even when it's parked up at a show. Would you keep it road-registered or go full track build?

#NissanGTR #GTRNismo #R35 #NissanNismo #CarShow #JapaneseCars #TrackMarc #Motorsport #CarPhotography #LeMans #GranTurismo
DSC_4601-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Two very different answers to the same question.

A white GT-R NISMO with that red splitter trim sits alongside a graphite 992 GT3, yellow calipers peeking out behind the rear wing. One's a Japanese touring car built for the track with motorsport DNA baked in, the other a purist's sports car that's barely road legal. Same grass, very different philosophies.

Which one are you walking home with?

#NissanGTR #GTNISMO #Porsche911 #GT3 #992GT3 #PorscheGT3 #SupercarMeet #CarShow #JDM #TrackMarc #MotorsportPhotography #Ireland
DSC_4606-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Paddock life is more than just the cars. A Prodrive pass, a lanyard, and La Dolce Vita serving up fresh Italian behind you, that's a proper way to spend a race weekend. What's your go-to paddock food stop?
DSC_4612-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
A 944 in Martini colours rolling through the paddock is always a proper sight. The G3OMAN plate is a nice touch on what was already one of Porsche's more underrated machines, the first to run a front-mounted, water-cooled four-pot built entirely in-house. The Cayenne shadowing it doesn't quite have the same energy, does it.

#Porsche944 #Porsche #MartiniLivery #PaddockLife #ClassicPorsche #TrackDay #CarShow #PorscheClassic #Motorsport #PorscheCommunity @trackmarcdotcom
DSC_4617-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Guards Red and dark wheels, the 964 doesn't need to move to command attention. This early 90s 911 Carrera is one of the most significant in the lineage, sharing less than 15% of its parts with the previous generation while keeping everything that made the 911 special. Seeing one in this colour, in this condition, is a proper treat. Which generation of air-cooled 911 do you reckon is the definitive one?
DSC_4619-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Guards Red, Cup wheels, and not a mark on it.

The 964 generation doesn't get enough credit. Coil springs, all-wheel drive on the Carrera 4, and a cleaner body than the cars that came before it, yet it still looks unmistakably like a 911. This one's presented perfectly.

Air-cooled or water-cooled, which generation is your favourite 911?

#Porsche #Porsche911 #964 #PorscheCup #GuardsRed #ClassicPorsche #CarShow #AirCooled #Trackmarc
DSC_4621-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Twenty years captured in a little shield on the glass.

This 964 Carrera 2S wears its 1992–2012 anniversary sticker with real quiet pride, a small detail that carries a lot of history. The 964 generation doesn't shout for attention, it earns it, and this one is no different.

Do you think the 964 is still the purest shape the 911 ever wore?

#Porsche #Porsche911 #964 #Carrera2S #ZwanzigJahre #PorscheClassic #CarShow #911Life #AirCooled #ClassicPorsche #trackmarcdotcom
DSC_4695-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Over a thousand horses, parked quietly under indoor lights.

The Aston Martin Valkyrie AMR Pro in that dark teal and black two-tone is something else up close. Built purely for the track, it corners at 3g on Pirelli slicks and carries a naturally aspirated Cosworth V12 that sounds like nothing else on four wheels. You don't need to see it moving to feel how serious it is.

Would you rather see this one static or flat out on circuit?
DSC_4698-Enhanced-NR.jpg: The Mk IV doesn't do road cars, only race wins. Ford Performance and Multimatic built this blue GT exclusively for competition, and that glowing pit board showing 6:15 is a reminder it belongs on a circuit, not a forecourt. Michelin Pilots, no compromise. What's your favourite Ford GT generation?
DSC_4699-Enhanced-NR.jpg: British Racing Green, naturally aspirated, and built to conquer Le Mans. The Aston Martin Valkyrie AMR Pro sits under the lights on static display, but every line of that closed-wheel bodywork tells you this thing was never meant to stand still. Over 1,000 bhp from a Cosworth V12, Pirelli slicks ready to go, and aero channelling that makes the road car look almost tame. What do you reckon sounds better, this V12 at full chat or any hybrid hypercar you care to name?
DSC_4700-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
British Racing Green on the most extreme Aston ever built.

The Valkyrie AMR Pro doesn't do things by half. A naturally aspirated Cosworth V12 pushing over 1,000 bhp, Akebono brakes that mean business, and Pirelli P Zeros with that unmistakable yellow script. Track-only, Red Bull Advanced Technologies underneath, and a colour that feels entirely right on it.

Would you take it in any other colour?

#AstonMartin #Valkyrie #AMRPro #BritishRacingGreen #Cosworth #V12 #TrackCar #Hypercar #Pirelli #Akebono #MotorShow #AstonMartinValkyrie #TrackMarcDotCom
DSC_4701-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Even the buggy gets the full McLaren treatment at Le Mans. Numbered 01 and decked out in team livery, this paddock runabout is part of the quietly impressive logistics machine that keeps a top endurance programme moving. An Iveco support truck parked alongside gives you a sense of the scale involved. What's your favourite bit of paddock life that most people walk straight past?
DSC_4206-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Sixty years of silence on the old Monza banking. Built in 1955, last raced in 1969, this crumbling cathedral of speed sits preserved inside the park, the autumn trees creeping ever closer to the concrete. It's one of those places you stand on and genuinely feel the weight of what happened here. Have you ever walked the banking yourself?

#Monza #AutodromoNazionaleMonza #MonzaBanking #MonzaHistoric #ItalianGP #MotorsportHistory #ClassicMotorsport #TrackPhotography #Motorsport #F1History #IrishPhotographer #TrackMarcDotCom
DSC_4228-Enhanced-NR.jpg:
Blue through the trees at the temple of speed. The Alpine cuts through Monza's parkland section, Ferrari flags waving in the grandstands behind, a reminder that this is very much enemy territory. One of the oldest circuits on the calendar, and it still doesn't get old. Do you think Monza deserves to stay on the F1 calendar forever?

#Monza #ItalianGP #Alpine #AlpineF1 #Formula1 #F1 #Motorsport #Autodromo #Tifosi #ItalianGrandPrix #F1Photography #MotorsportPhotography #trackmarcdotcom
DSC_4266-Enhanced-NR.jpg: Even in F1, someone needs a brew. Behind the glamour and the pit wall drama, there are hundreds of people in orange suits keeping this whole circus running, and they need a breather too. Who's your favourite unsung hero at a race weekend?
DSC_4557.jpg: Mic in hand, paddock stories flowing at Le Mans. Some of the best moments here happen away from the cars entirely. What's the best paddock chat you've ever caught on camera?
DSC_4624.jpg: White and red LMP2 hunting through this Le Mans corner. The Cadillac boards mark one of the trickiest bends on the Circuit de la Sarthe. Which corner do you reckon separates the quick drivers from the rest?
DSC_4625.jpg: IDEC Sport's #28 Oreca chasing points at Le Mans. Les Echos and Le Parisien along the flanks, running hard in P2. Are you an LMP2 fan or is Hypercar the only class that matters to you?
DSC_4626.jpg: CrowdStrike's #4 Oreca digging into this corner at Le Mans. Cybersecurity money funding endurance racing is one of the stranger sponsor crossovers out there. What's the most unexpected sponsor you've seen on an LMP2 car?
DSC_4627.jpg: Mobil 1 red streaking past at Le Mans. Golden hour does something to these LMP2 shots that no filter can match. What time of day makes for the best trackside photos in your experience?
DSC_4630.jpg: Shopify green leading the charge through this Cadillac corner. Two Orecas nose to tail, fighting for track position at Le Mans. Do you back the car in front or the one closing in?
DSC_4632.jpg: Yellow and green wing.vc Oreca cutting through the dusk at Le Mans. The light at this hour is why endurance racing photographs so well. What's your favourite livery colour combo in LMP2 right now?
DSC_4635.jpg: Marshals holding position while the field streams past at Le Mans. Every orange overall out there is a volunteer keeping this race safe. Have you ever marshalled at a motorsport event yourself?
DSC_4638.jpg: Heart of Racing's #27 Aston Martin Vantage GT3 under the lights at Le Mans. That dark blue livery looks the business as the sun goes down. Which GT3 livery is doing it for you this season?
DSC_4641.jpg: Mobil 1 Oreca sweeping past the Cadillac boards at Le Mans. Two marshals watching every lap from behind the barrier. What's the most underrated job at a race weekend?
DSC_4645.jpg: Shopify green under the first of the floodlights at Le Mans. This corner never gets old no matter how many times a lap it repeats. Would you rather shoot day running or night running at Le Mans?
DSC_4648.jpg: wing.vc Oreca cutting through near darkness at Le Mans. Headlights against black trees make for one of the moodiest shots of the whole weekend. Night shots or golden hour, which do you prefer?
DSC_4649.jpg: Same wing.vc Oreca, different bend, same low light magic at Le Mans. The cockpit glow against the dusk is hard to beat. What's the hardest part about shooting cars in low light?
DSC_4650.jpg: VistaJet's Ferrari 296 GT3 catching the last of the light at Le Mans. Richard Mille backing on the flanks, marshals watching from trackside. Ferrari or Porsche, who's got the better GT3 this year?
DSC_4652.jpg: BMW M4 GT3 in full BMW M Motorsport colours at dusk. That orange and light blue combo stands out even as the light fades. What's your favourite BMW motorsport livery of all time?
DSC_4653.jpg: Porsche 911 GT3 R leading a rival through the twilight at Le Mans. Both cars picked out by their own headlights as the evening closes in. Who do you reckon comes out on top in this fight?
DSC_4654.jpg: Aston Martin Vantage GT3 cutting through the near-dark at Le Mans. Headlights doing all the work as the last light disappears. Do you shoot manual or let the camera choose in conditions like this?
DSC_4659.jpg: Lexus RC F GT3 in Alizoa colours through the Cadillac corner. Yellow and dark blue is a combination you don't see enough of. What's the most underrated GT3 manufacturer out there right now?
DSC_4660.jpg: Oreca #10 rounding the bend as the trees turn black at Le Mans. Headlights on, chasing every last bit of light before full dark. How many hours in do you reckon this shot was taken?
DSC_4666.jpg: Shopify green back past the Cadillac boards at Le Mans. Same car, new angle, still one of the best liveries in LMP2. Do you prefer following one car all race or chasing variety?
DSC_4667.jpg: Mascot's white and purple Oreca cutting through the mist at Le Mans. Headlights blazing as the visibility drops with the light. What's the trickiest weather you've ever tried to shoot in?
DSC_4668.jpg: Black and green LMP2 disappearing into near darkness at Le Mans. A few more minutes and this corner will be lit by headlights alone. Do you stay out shooting once it's fully dark?
DSC_4688.jpg: Heart of Racing's Aston Martin hunting down the #91 Porsche at Le Mans. This is the kind of GT3 battle that makes the whole race worth watching. Who are you backing in this fight, Aston or Porsche?
DSC_4690.jpg: Ford Mustang GT3 #77 firing out of the corner at Le Mans. Ford Racing back in GT3 and it still sounds the part. Are you enjoying Ford's return to sports car racing?
DSC_4710.jpg: Hypercars trading paint through this chicane at Le Mans. Red, yellow and white against a white rival, right on the TotalEnergies boards. Which Hypercar manufacturer are you rooting for this year?
DSC_4714.jpg: Cadillac's #12 Hertz liveried Hypercar streaking past the Rolex fencing at Le Mans. That cream and black combo is one of the sharper liveries on the grid. What do you make of Cadillac's Hypercar effort so far?
DSC_4719.jpg: Hypercar glowing red under the Rolex bridge as the brakes bite at Le Mans. Dusk at the Sarthe circuit does something no daytime shot can match. What's your favourite spot on this track for evening photos?
DSC_4720.jpg: A dark Hypercar slipping past the pit lane as night takes over at Le Mans. Manthey's 30th anniversary support truck parked up trackside. Thirty years in sports car racing, what's changed the most in your view?
DSC_4721.jpg: Heart of Racing's Valkyrie #007 catching the last of the daylight at Le Mans. That V12 scream is one of the best sounds in Hypercar. What's it like hearing a Valkyrie on track for the first time?
DSC_4722.jpg: BMW's M Hybrid V8 through Hyperpole qualifying at Le Mans. One lap, no room for error, the whole session up on the big screen. Do you find qualifying or the race itself more tense to watch?
DSC_4723.jpg: Hypercar crossing the line as the Rolex clock ticks down at Le Mans. Every second here counts towards the overall result. What's the most nerve wracking part of watching a Hypercar qualifying lap?
DSC_4725.jpg: Car 17 catching the floodlights as daylight gives up at Le Mans. This transition from dusk to full dark is one of the best windows of the whole race. Are you a fan of the floodlit hours at Le Mans?
DSC_4728.jpg: Toyota's #8 GR010 Hybrid crossing the line as the light fades at Le Mans. Denso backing on the flanks of one of the most decorated Hypercars in the paddock. Can Toyota keep their Le Mans run going this year?
DSC_4731.jpg: Heart of Racing's Valkyrie #007 as dusk falls over the Sarthe. That green liveried V12 Hypercar never gets old on camera. Where does the Valkyrie rank among your favourite Hypercars?
DSC_4732.jpg: Genesis Magma Racing's #19 pulling away, tail lights glowing red at Le Mans. That fire inspired livery looks even better lit up like this. What do you make of Genesis stepping into Hypercar?
DSC_4734.jpg: Another Hertz liveried Cadillac past the packed grandstands at Le Mans. The crowd here never seems to thin out no matter the hour. What's the atmosphere like watching from these stands, have you been?
DSC_4743.jpg: Heart of Racing's Valkyrie #009 sat in the pit lane at Le Mans. Roman, Alex and Marco named right across the roof. Do you like seeing driver names on the bodywork like this?
DSC_4749.jpg: Eni's Ferrari 499P leading a pack of LMP2s down the pit straight. The Rolex clock and a packed grandstand setting the scene at Le Mans. Is Ferrari your pick for outright honours this year?
DSC_4751.jpg: Genesis Magma Racing's #19 running sixth and holding station at Le Mans. That board on the tail tells you everything about how the race is going. Would sixth be a result Genesis should be happy with here?
DSC_4753.jpg: The view down Le Mans pit lane from the Dunlop bridge at dusk. Every pit board and garage lit up as the cars come through. Is this the best vantage point on the whole circuit, in your opinion?
DSC_4755.jpg: Heart of Racing's Valkyrie #009 getting worked on in the pit lane. Green overalls, careful hands, no room for mistakes at this level. What's the longest you've ever waited to watch a pit stop unfold?
DSC_4760.jpg: Alpine's #35 A424 running 13th past the TotalEnergies boards at Le Mans. Alpine are still the only French manufacturer to have won this race outright, back in 1978. Do you think Alpine can climb the order from here?
DSC_4763.jpg: Both Heart of Racing Valkyries nose to tail in the pit lane. Cars 007 and 009 side by side is a proper sight at Le Mans. Which of the two liveries do you prefer?
DSC_4766.jpg: The Automobile Club de l'Ouest tower watching over a packed house at Le Mans. Rolex branding wrapped around every level of the place that runs this race. Have you ever been up in the race control tower?
DSC_4832.jpg: Inside BMW M Team WRT's garage as the crew works through the night. Kevin Magnussen up on the screen while the car gets prepped for its next stint. What's it like watching a night pit stop up close?
DSC_4843.jpg: VistaJet's Ferrari #83 surrounded by its crew mid pit stop at Le Mans. A fire marshal stood ready, extinguisher in hand, just in case. How many people do you reckon it takes to run a pit stop like this?
DSC_4849.jpg: Wayne Taylor Racing's Cadillac #101 getting serviced in the pit lane. Ricky Taylor, Jordan Taylor and Albuquerque all named on the windscreen. Are you following the WTR Cadillac programme this season?
